memorydb.cluster.ACLName.Docs: The name of the Access Control List to associate with the cluster.

memorydb.cluster.AutoMinorVersionUpgrade.Docs: When set to true, the cluster will automatically receive minor engine version upgrades after launch.

memorydb.cluster.EngineVersion.Docs: The Redis engine version used by the cluster.

memorydb.cluster.FinalSnapshotName.Docs: The user-supplied name of a final cluster snapshot. This is the unique name that identifies the snapshot. MemoryDB creates the snapshot, and then deletes the cluster immediately afterward.

memorydb.cluster.KmsKeyId.Docs: The ID of the KMS key used to encrypt the cluster.

memorydb.cluster.MaintenanceWindow.Docs: Specifies the weekly time range during which maintenance on the cluster is performed. It is specified as a range in the format `ddd:hh24:mi-ddd:hh24:mi` (24H Clock UTC). The minimum maintenance window is a 60 minute period.

memorydb.cluster.NumReplicasPerShard.Docs: The number of replicas to apply to each shard.

memorydb.cluster.NumShards.Docs: The number of shards in the cluster.

memorydb.cluster.ParameterGroupName.Docs: The name of the parameter group used by the cluster.

memorydb.cluster.Port.Docs: The port used by the cluster.

memorydb.cluster.SecurityGroupIds.Docs: A list of security group names to associate with this cluster.

memorydb.cluster.SnapshotArns.Docs: A list of Amazon Resource Names (ARN) that uniquely identify the RDB snapshot files stored in Amazon S3. The snapshot files are used to populate the new cluster. The Amazon S3 object name in the ARN cannot contain any commas.

memorydb.cluster.SnapshotName.Docs: The name of a snapshot from which to restore data into the new cluster. The snapshot status changes to restoring while the new cluster is being created.

memorydb.cluster.SnapshotRetentionLimit.Docs: The number of days for which MemoryDB retains automatic snapshots before deleting them. For example, if you set SnapshotRetentionLimit to 5, a snapshot that was taken today is retained for 5 days before being deleted.

memorydb.cluster.SnapshotWindow.Docs: The daily time range (in UTC) during which MemoryDB begins taking a daily snapshot of your shard. Example: 05:00-09:00 If you do not specify this parameter, MemoryDB automatically chooses an appropriate time range.

memorydb.cluster.SnsTopicArn.Docs: When you pass the logical ID of this resource to the intrinsic `Ref` function, Ref returns the ARN of the SNS topic, such as `arn:aws:memorydb:us-east-1:123456789012:mySNSTopic`

memorydb.cluster.SnsTopicStatus.Docs: The SNS topic must be in Active status to receive notifications.

memorydb.cluster.SubnetGroupName.Docs: The name of the subnet group used by the cluster.

memorydb.cluster.Tags.Docs: An array of key-value pairs to apply to this resource.  For more information, see [Tag](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-properties-resource-tags.html).

memorydb.cluster.TLSEnabled.Docs: A flag to indicate if In-transit encryption is enabled.
